summary: FPV drone units from both sides reported high-tempo operations in March 2026, with the Russian "Rubikon" Center claiming 88 fixed-wing UAV intercepts on March 7 and 107 on March 6 in the Donbas and Sumy directions, targeting Ukrainian personnel and airframes. Ukrainian forces, including Lasar’s Group NSU, repelled mechanized assaults on Novopavlivka and Dobropillia fronts, destroying enemy tanks. Technical developments highlighted Iran’s subsonic loitering air defense missile, Missile 358, which reportedly downed Israeli Hermes-900 UAVs using thermal imaging homing. Russian operators also demonstrated Lancet loitering munition strikes on grouped targets despite EW interference. Ukrainian drone operations are being consolidated under the new Mission Control digital system to improve battlefield coordination. Analysts noted a decline in Russian troop density within Ukrainian drone strike range, complicating efforts to meet high monthly attrition targets.
